what_it_does: Defines the Virtual Hub SharePanel: a right-side aside (reusing the vh-info-panel shell) showing a shareable hub URL and a list of share actions (Email, Copy link, Embed, Download PDF brochure), each with an inline SVG icon, plus a note that recipients see the hub at its current published stage. Includes a ShIcon helper. Registered as window.SharePanel.
observations: Reuses className 'vh-info-panel' (shares the InfoPanel shell rather than a dedicated share class).
ShIcon helper renders array path 'd' strings in an 18x18 viewBox.
Displays a constructed URL https://conceptv.io/panotours/<accent>acme</accent>/{hub?.id || 'tower-east'} with hardcoded 'acme' tenant and a hub-id fallback.
Four share rows (Email, Copy link, Embed, Download PDF brochure) — none have onClick handlers wired.
Closing note paragraph uses inline style with token vars (var(--font-body), var(--fg-muted)).
standouts: The note 'Recipients of this link will see the Hub at its current published stage' encodes a publish-stage sharing semantic.
Mixes inline-style token vars on the note while the rest relies on vh-* classes.
conventions: Follows vh-* class naming and global-window registration.
Inline style on the note uses var(--token) tokens, partially aligning with the design-token convention; other styling is class-based.
Reuses the InfoPanel shell class — single shell, multiple panel contents.
concerns: Hardcoded tenant 'acme' in the share URL and an unwired share action list (no handlers).
One-off inline style block instead of a class for the note — a small drift from the class-based pattern in this kit.
notes: The right-side Share panel of the Virtual Hub — shows the hub link plus Email/Copy/Embed/PDF actions; URL tenant is hardcoded and actions are presentational stubs.
questions: Should the 'acme' tenant segment come from hub/tenant data rather than being hardcoded?
Are the share rows intended to be wired to handlers later, or is this a static mockup of the share affordances?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the README's SharePanel row (share by link / email / social); 'social' is represented as Email/Copy/Embed/PDF rather than named social networks.
apparent_themes: Minimal dark overlay panel reusing a common shell
intention_signals: Aims to let a viewer share the immersive hub via link, email, embed, or brochure, with clarity that the shared view reflects the current published stage.
novelty: The publish-stage note and the panotour share-URL structure (tenant/hub) as a sharing affordance.
contribution: Contributes the share-panel pattern: a shareable hub URL display plus an icon list of share modes (email/copy/embed/PDF) and a publish-stage disclosure, reusing the info-panel shell.
summary_for_embedding: React SharePanel component for the Virtual Hub showing a shareable hub URL and Email/Copy/Embed/PDF share actions with inline icons in a right-side aside.
intention_for_embedding: To let viewers share a published immersive hub by link, email, embed, or brochure with publish-stage clarity.
